title: Practical Linux Firewalls: iptables, nftables, UFW & firewalld
description: A concise practical guide to Linux firewall tools with commands and example outputs. Covers iptables, nftables, ufw, and firewalld plus best practices.
author: E-Learning Platform

items.label: iptables version
items.code:
user@server:~$ iptables --version
iptables v1.8.9 (nf_tables)

items.label: nftables version
items.code:
user@server:~$ nft --version
nftables v0.9.8 (nf_tables)

items.label: UFW status (Ubuntu)
items.code:
user@server:~$ sudo ufw status
Status: inactive

items.label: firewalld state (RHEL/CentOS)
items.code:
user@server:~$ sudo firewall-cmd --state
running

items.label: iptables (filter table, non-verbose)
items.code:
user@server:~$ sudo iptables -L
Chain INPUT (policy ACCEPT)
target     prot opt source               destination

Chain FORWARD (policy ACCEPT)
target     prot opt source               destination

Chain OUTPUT (policy ACCEPT)
target     prot opt source               destination

items.label: iptables (verbose)
items.code:
user@server:~$ sudo iptables -L -v
Chain INPUT (policy ACCEPT 0 packets, 0 bytes)
 pkts bytes target     prot opt in     out     source               destination
  1234  123K ACCEPT     all  --  lo     any     anywhere             anywhere
   567 45678 ACCEPT     tcp  --  any    any     anywhere             anywhere             tcp dpt:ssh

items.label: nftables (ruleset)
firewalld (list active config)
items.code:
user@server:~$ sudo firewall-cmd --list-all
public (active)
  target: default
  services: ssh dhcpv6-client
  ports: 80/tcp 443/tcp

items.label: UFW (status verbose)
items.code:
user@server:~$ sudo ufw status verbose
Status: active
Logging: on (low)
To                         Action      From
--                         ------      ----
22/tcp                     ALLOW       Anywhere
80/tcp                     ALLOW       Anywhere

items.label: Allow SSH (iptables)
items.code: user@server:~$ sudo iptables -A INPUT -p tcp --dport 22 -m conntrack --ctstate NEW,ESTABLISHED -j ACCEPT

items.label: Allow SSH (ufw)
items.code:
user@server:~$ sudo ufw allow 22/tcp
Rules updated

items.label: Allow HTTP & HTTPS (firewall-cmd)
items.code:
user@server:~$ sudo firewall-cmd --permanent --add-service=http
success
user@server:~$ sudo firewall-cmd --permanent --add-service=https
success
user@server:~$ sudo firewall-cmd --reload
success

items.label: Allow port with nftables
items.code: user@server:~$ sudo nft add rule inet filter input tcp dport 80 accept

items.label: Drop a hostile IP (iptables)
items.code: user@server:~$ sudo iptables -A INPUT -s 203.0.113.51 -j DROP

items.label: Enable IP forwarding
items.code:
user@server:~$ sudo sysctl -w net.ipv4.ip_forward=1
net.ipv4.ip_forward = 1

items.label: MASQUERADE (iptables NAT)
items.code: user@server:~$ sudo iptables -t nat -A POSTROUTING -o eth0 -j MASQUERADE

items.label: Save (Debian/Ubuntu)
items.code:
user@server:~$ sudo netfilter-persistent save
Saving firewall rules to /etc/iptables/rules.v4

items.label: Save with iptables-save
items.code:
user@server:~$ sudo iptables-save > /etc/iptables/rules.v4
user@server:~$ sudo iptables-restore < /etc/iptables/rules.v4

items.label: List rules (iptables)
items.code: user@server:~$ sudo iptables -L -v

items.label: Show ruleset (nft)
items.code: user@server:~$ sudo nft list ruleset
